title: blumea chinensis var. riparia
intro: Blumea chinensis var. riparia, also known as Chinese camphor daisy or riparian whiteflower, is a plant species in the daisy family. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Blumea chinensis var. riparia include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions.
